commentaries.author: Ambrose of Milan
commentaries.content: I know that you [Emperor Theodosius I] are Godfearing, merciful, gentle and calm, that you have the faith and fear of God in your heart, but often some things escape our notice. Some people have a zeal for God but not according to knowledge. Care must be taken … lest this condition steal upon pious souls.

commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: For, being ignorant of God's righteousness, and seeking to establish their own, they have not submitted themselves to the righteousness of God."

commentaries.author: Gennadius of Constantinople
commentaries.content: Having once been one of them himself, Paul understood and bore witness that the Jews fought against the gospel out of zeal for God, yet it was a zeal uninformed by true knowledge. .

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: According to knowledge The Jews ran with ardour in the paths of the law, but saw not whither they were going; they followed the law, but did not know whither it conducted them. (Calmet)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Should not the Jews be excused if they have erred because of some misguided zeal? Notice how Paul goes on to remove this excuse from them.

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: For "says he, "being ignorant of (the righteousness of) God, and going about to establish their own righteousness, they have not submitted themselves unto the righteousness of God; for Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to every one that believeth."

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
